            Catholic education - belong to something greater                      Living our faith through actions that inspire greatnessOur schools nurture purpose, character and compassion. Students bring their faith to life through service and care for others that reflects values that will continue to shape their lives beyond school. More  Learning that ignites excellenceA commitment to high-quality education and diverse opportunities allowing students to experience personal learning success. Excellence in learning is guided/driven by high standards that meet the individual needs of students to achieve, improve and grow. More  Everyone belongsLifelong connections are nurtured through peer support and pastoral care. Every learner is known and valued and our commitment to accessibility ensures that affordable fees, scholarships and fee support options help every family feel they truly belong. More  Opportunities to growWhether through leadership, sport, creative arts, trade pathways or service, every student is offered the opportunity to explore their unique strengths and discover their
                                            calling. With access to innovative digital learning, STEM and creative programs, students are prepared for the future while being supported to thrive - not only in academic achievement, but in integrity, vision and drive. More   The best school ever! I have three kids attending St Oliver's and I am grateful they are there becoming safe, respectful learners. Vivian Orji - Parent, St Oliver's Harris Park  We encourage our students to have the courage, strength, and confidence to proudly embrace their culture and continue this journey for the sustainability of the oldest continuing culture on earth. Julie Waddell - Jarara Indigenous Education Unit  Our school has a good sense of discipline and helps students branch out and really choose their future. It teaches you early on to find your own ideas and solutions and be your own man. Emilio Nacua - Student, Parramatta Marist High  The best school ever! I have three kids attending St Oliver's and I am grateful they are there becoming safe, respectful learners. It teaches you early on to find your own ideas and solutions and be your own man. Emilio Nacua - Student, Parramatta Marist High  The best school ever! I have three kids attending St Oliver's and I am grateful they are there becoming safe, respectful learners. Vivian Orji - Parent, St Oliver's Harris Park  123  Our commitment to every student We believe every child has the potential for greatness. At Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese (CSPD), greatness is found in the growth of a student who is encouraged to thrive, not just in academic achievement, but in character, purpose, and belonging.  You’re welcome here. That’s what makes Catholic education truly special. Our faith and values shape a caring, inclusive community where every student and family is valued. Across our 80 schools, we share a commitment to enrich the lives of our students, through education grounded in excellence, inclusivity and Catholic tradition.           About Belong to something greater - education grounded in excellence, inclusivity and Catholic tradition.1 Our Story With 80 great schools across Western Sydney and the Blue Mountains, Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese serves one of the fastest growing areas of NSW. We are committed to excellence in education so that every student in every one of our schools has the opportunity to thrive. We do this by maximising each student's potential, with evidence-based teaching practices, fit-for-purpose learning environments, and targeted student support catered to all learning needs.  Find out more  Our Story Our Leadership A Board of Directors led by Chair Elizabeth Crouch AM provides strategic oversight to the organisation. Executive Director Jack de Groot leads a team of directors responsible for the execution of the CSPD system strategy. Meet our Board 
                            Meet the Chief Executive Officer Meet the Executive General Managers Our Leadership Our Bishop Most Reverend Vincent Long Van Nguyen
                            OFM Conv DD STL Fourth Bishop of Parramatta Appointed on 5 May 2016, Bishop Vincent was installed as the fourth Bishop of Parramatta at St Patrick’s Cathedral, Parramatta on 16 June 2016. Find out about the Diocese of Parramatta Our Bishop Our strategy and policies Our strategy is based on six strategic pillars - Mission, People, Quality, Relevance, Sustainability and Trust. We are focused on enriching the lives of our students through education grounded in excellence, inclusivity and Catholic Tradition and fostering a high performance culture that recognises and values the strengths of our students and staff. We provide advice and recommendations on educational and administrative policies and priorities, and monitor policy implementation. Find out more Our strategy and policies Building child safe communities We are committed to providing children and young people with school environments where they are safe, informed and participate, and where the adult community works together with them towards making this happen. How we protect child safety Building child safe communities About Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese A Catholic education in the Diocese of Parramatta is innovative, personalised and relevant to the needs of today's learner. Our system of schools is a work of the Church, under the leadership of our Bishop and in collaboration with priests, parents, students and staff. Our schools are funded by the Australian and NSW governments as well as through modest school fees and fundraising.  Frequently Asked Questions Compliance and reporting Catholic school funding About Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese 
            Explore our 80 schools                     Catholic schools have a history of academic excellence and as providers of quality education. We celebrate the achievements of every student and encourage them to reach their full potential. Catholic education is affordable and accessible for every family. Meet our Chief Executive Officer Meet our Board of Directors From the beginning of 2023 Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese had a new name, Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese Ltd. In the spirit of leading together, the organisation also introduced a new governance framework.           Careers Explore potential career opportunities at CSPD and kickstart your future with us.1 
            Why work for us?                     Every one of our employees supports our mission to serve our students, staff and communities, with quality education, fulfilling employment and impactful change. We need the right people to make the greatest difference. If you strive to make a genuine contribution to enriching the lives of students through education grounded in excellence, inclusivity and Catholic tradition, then a career in Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese may be the right move for you. Search for positions vacant  
            Current opportunities                     TeachingWe offer rewarding teaching roles in our primary and secondary schools, and specialist roles too. Learn more   Casual TeachingSign up today to start receiving notifications of casual teaching opportunities. Learn more   Non-Teaching RolesBring your diverse experience to our team and help us to provide valuable support and expertise to our large network of schools. Learn more   LeadershipSchool leaders embrace change and new ideas to drive contemporary learning in Catholic schools. Learn more   IT TraineeshipsLooking for a career in IT? Get a head start by joining our IT Traineeship Program. Learn more  
            What we offer                     25% school fees discounts for CSPD staffOur staff play a pivotal role in our school communities, fostering our love of learning and commitment to academic excellence. We aim to attract the best talent and give back to our dedicated and valued employees, which is why staff can now receive a 25% discount off the Diocesan Tuition fee and Building Levy for their children who are enrolled into a CSPD school. A Teacher Scholarship ProgramThis exciting program is aimed at current Year 12 students at one of CSPD’s 25 secondary schools looking to commence a full-time Primary or Secondary teaching degree next year at an Australian university. Our future teachers will be able to receive financial assistance, apply for paid employment in our schools and access professional development, mentoring and coaching support. Find out more 
            A staff wellbeing framework that supports respectful, collaborative relationships                     Our employees - teachers, leaders and support staff - are our greatest asset. The CSPD Staff Wellbeing Framework provides effective practices that we know can help us all in our shared work of making our workplaces and schools healthy and safe for everyone. The Framework focuses on those things we can influence as colleagues and team members. We share responsibility for creating workplaces that are supportive and inclusive. Wellbeing is such an important focus for all of us.     Executive Director Jack De Groot is joined by Director Wellbeing Dr Greg Elliott to discuss the Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese Staff Wellbeing Framework.   I am really excited about my future at CSPD because I know that I can really grow as an educator and this profession will lead to so many possibilities and opportunities.  Stephanie Allegra  - Year 2 Classroom Teacher, St Patrick's Primary Blacktown  I truly believe that CSPD is a great place to work because we embrace equity and inclusion and promote education for all. Cristina Cignarella - Religious Education Coordinator, St Francis of Assisi Glendenning  Our purpose and our people are some of the many reasons CSPD is a great place to work. Erin Morin - Safeguarding Senior Manager CathEd Parra  I am really excited about my future at CSPD because I know that I can really grow as an educator and this profession will lead to so many possibilities and opportunities.  Stephanie Allegra  - Year 2 Classroom Teacher, St Patrick's Primary B

Haven’t visited yet? There’s still time to explore how your local Catholic school empowers every child to strive for greatness! 🌟

📍 Find an Open Day near you: https://info.parra.catholic.edu.au/open-days-listing

📸 Xavier College Lllandilo, Our Lady of the Rosary Primary School Kellyville, St John XXIII Catholic College Schofields, St Bernadette's Primary Dundas Valley, Patrician Brothers' College, Blacktown, St Canice's Primary Katoomba Read more 4 Mar 2026 From CSPD Facebook FREE Parent Event: Raising Resilient Kids in a Fast-Paced World 💬

Parenting in today’s fast-paced world isn’t always easy. Join author and parent educator Gen Muir for practical tips on helping children build resilience, confidence and emotional wellbeing.

You’ll walk away with helpful ideas to support your child through big emotions, friendship challenges, sibling conflict and the ups and downs of growing up.

📅 Tuesday 24 March
⏰ 7:00pm – 8:30pm (doors open 6:30pm)
📍 St John XXIII Catholic College, Stanhope Gardens

Come along, connect with other parents and learn more about CSPD’s new Family Partnership Council - FPC.

🔗 Register now: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1E4WVkcLEfGH7Jf59uuo7qi-HfXNtVwKQxeeCdv9-Z38/viewform?ts=69a8d620&edit_requested=true

Connected Parenting Read more a day ago From CSPD Facebook 🙏✨ A Special Visit from the Bishop!

The community at St John Paul II Catholic College, Schofields & Nirimba welcomed Vincent Long Van Nguyen, Bishop of the Catholic Diocese of Parramatta, for a memorable visit.

Bishop Vincent spent time engaging with students and staff, listening to the many ways they bring the Gospel to life in their learning and service. He was particularly inspired by the students involved in the Diocesan Catechist Programme, who share their faith with younger students in local state primary schools, as well as the incredible work of the Faith in Action team in promoting social justice initiatives.

As we celebrate Catholic Schools Week, visits like these highlight what makes our school communities so special - faith lived out in action, a spirit of compassion and students striving to make a positive difference. 💛 Read more 7 Mar 2026 From CSPD Facebook HIGH ENERGY AT ENCOUNTER! ⚡🎶

Our Year 6 students had an amazing time at Encounter, a special formation day where they deepened their faith alongside students from other CSPD schools - and just by these photos, you can see the joy, energy and sense of community that filled the room! 🥹

Throughout the day, students engaged in meaningful conversations, reflected on their faith journeys, sang together in worship and shared a beautiful time of Adoration.

A standout moment for many (students and teachers alike!) was meeting the inspiring Gen Bryant, whose music encouraged everyone to connect

          Parents as Partners All CSPD schools welcome parents as a key partner in their child's schooling. Find out how you can get involved at your local school.1 Building a warm and welcoming community is extremely important in every Catholic school. Our schools view parents and carers as important partners in their child’s education and are committed to strengthening the connection between home and school.  We welcome our students and their families to not just enrol in a school, but to join a dynamic learning  community where everyone has a role to play. Building a warm and welcoming community is extremely important in every Catholic school. Partnership with families is at the heart of everything we do We want every family to feel confident and supported when engaging with our schools. That’s why all our schools follow the Family and School Partnership Principles. These explain how we can all work together in a positive and respectful way, including how to raise an enquiry, concern or complaint. It is important that we engage with one another in a way that is respectful and supportive of the wellbeing of all. Read our Family and School Partnership Principles for more Partnership with families is at the heart of everything we do Every family belongs, every voice matters The Family Partnership Council (FPC) is CSPD’s official Diocesan parent body.  A positive, simple and practical way for families to contribute to conversations across the Diocese of Parramatta. Find out more and get involved  Every family belongs, every voice matters 
            Parent groups in our schools                     Joining a parent group is a great way of getting involved in the life of your child’s school and playing an active part in their education.       
            Ask your school today if they have a parent group you can join!                      Find a school  
            Translation and interpreting service                     For families who need help with translation when communicating with their child's school a free telephone service is available. Learn more  
            Find out what’s happening in the Diocese of Parramatta                     Stay ConnectedConnect with us. Be welcomed. Be valued. Stay informed. Keep up-to-date with all the news, events and initiatives shaping learning across Catholic Schools Parramatta Diocese. Learn more   Term DatesHave a look at the Catholic schools term dates. School term dates and holidays are generally the same as government schools. Learn more   Transforming LearningDiscover how Catholic schools in the Diocese of Parramatta foster imagination and innovation from pre-school to Year 12 and beyond. Find out more    Building child safe communities All schools in the Diocese of Parramatta are committed to providing children and young people with safe, informed, and participatory environments, where the adult community collaborates with them to achieve this goal. We promote respect, kindness, acceptance, and patience, urging parents to model these values at school as their child's primary teacher.  To further ensure safety, a range of checks and undertakings are required for individuals working for or providing services to Catholic Schools in the Parramatta Diocese to reduce potential risks. How CSPD schools protect child safety Volunteer and contractor training Family & School Partnership Principles Policy   My daughters love attending St Lukes and being part of the St Lukes Community. We love the school's unique and innovative approach to learning.My daughters love attending St Lukes and being part of the St Lukes Community. We love the school's unique and innovative approach to learning. Tamara Smith - Parent, St Luke's Marsden Park1

            FAQs                     How many schools are part of CSPD?  The Diocese of Parramatta is located in one of the fastest growing areas of New South Wales. The diocese is west of Sydney and reaches from Dundas Valley, west to Katoomba, south to Luddenham, and north to Richmond. There are 80 Catholic systemic schools in the diocese (56 primary and 24 secondary), 25 out of school hours care centres, 5 early learning centres and 2 trade training centres with 43,000 students and 4,500 staff. Our story   Find a school  How are CSPD schools different from other schools?  Catholic schools are extremely popular. In fact, they enrol about 20 per cent of Australian school students. Catholic schools have a great reputation: we offer value for money, share good values and get excellent results!  Many parents say they have made their educational choice for reasons such as: Catholic schools are faith-based places that are grounded in Christian values
                                                        The Catholic faith is not just taught in Religious Education classes but is experienced within the total culture of the school. Christian values are up front and explicit. Catholic schools are intrinsically connected to the parish in which they reside and are an integral part of the mission and life of the Church. Our Catholic Tradition    Religious Education Catholic schools strive to achieve very high standards of education
                                                        Expectations of achievement and conduct are high. While there is a focus on the individual child, a broad curriculum caters for a wide range of interests and abilities. Learning They are safe yet challenging places
                                                        Relationships are warm and friendly. Parents, students, teachers and pastors see themselves as working in partnership. The children belong to a community that really cares about each one of them. Parents as Partners    Wellbeing and Safety  How do I make a complaint?  We value parents as partners and welcome feedback from our community.  If you have a concern or complaint, please refer to the following guidelines to help you determine the most appropriate course of action. Download guidelines  Do CSPD schools offer the same curriculum as other schools?  Catholic schools teach the same curriculum as government schools. Expectations of achievement and conduct are high. With a strong focus on the individual, a broad curriculum caters for a wide range of interests and abilities. The curriculum covers: Religious Education Mathematics Science Technology and Applied Studies (TAS) English Human Society and its Environment (HSIE) Creative and Practical Arts Personal Development Health and Physical Education Languages other than English Secondary schools also offer a range of vocational education courses, some of whi
